Validated across pico-scale, nano, and micro runs: mixing Question/Answer pairs into pretraining works; the separate finetune stage collapsed tiny models into repetition. The collapse was later root-caused to an off-by-one bug in the finetune target construction (a copy objective), now fixed; whether the separate stage is genuinely worse once the bug is gone is pending a real run.

The pretrain stage renders instruction pairs as light Question/Answer text and mixes them into the corpus at instruction_fraction of tokens, teaching instruction-following jointly with fluency. The finetune stage continues training on the same pairs and format afterward. Empirically, the base pretrained model stays fluent and referent-free, but the finetuned checkpoint degenerates into repetitive, low-grammar output in three independent runs, despite eliminating the earlier suspected causes (role control tokens, format mismatch).

Confirmed instances: (1) an early 60M-parameter run at a bad tokens-per-parameter ratio, (2) the nano scale-ladder run (report_pretrain grammar 8.18 versus report_sft grammar 1.7, with literal repeated-character output), (3) the micro run showing coherent, referent-free behavior in the interactive pretrain-model session, consistent with the same collapse pattern though no separate sft report was surfaced for it. The mechanism was traced to a concrete bug rather than only training dynamics: render_pair_example in data.py built labels aligned to the same index as the input tokens instead of the next token, while the model computes cross entropy of the logits at position i against target i with no internal shift (the convention the packed pretraining data follows). The separate finetune stage was therefore the only path training a copy objective (predict the current answer token from itself), which produces exactly the observed repeated-token collapse, and explains why co-trained instruction following (which goes through the correctly shifted packed path) worked while the separate stage did not. The bug is fixed. Catastrophic forgetting from a second phase with no pretraining replay remains a plausible secondary factor for a tiny model, but is no longer the leading explanation. Decision (superseding the earlier recommendation to make finetune opt-in): keep the finetune stage as a default and improve it gradually, since SFT experiments are cheap relative to data generation and the scale-world runner (node 43) now trains and evaluates finetune at every rung on each run, producing a report_sft at pico, nano, micro, and full. That gives repeated, low-cost signal to iterate on the finetuning approach (for example replaying pretraining data during finetuning to prevent the forgetting that drives the collapse, or lighter-touch adaptation) rather than removing the stage. All the collapse evidence also predates the referent relaxation and the multi-domain content broadening, so whether the collapse persists on the new corpus is itself one of the experiments to run.
